attacking_style: Launches decisive solo attacks from breakaways on technical descents in final kilometers, exploiting hesitation among chasers rather than pure power moves.
team_tactics: Operates independently in long-range breakaways with minimal visible team support; relies on self-positioning and reading chase group dynamics.
terrain_preference: Technical descents from established breakaway positions. Shows resilience on repeated climbs but strikes on downhill finales where tactical awareness matters more than raw climbing.
vulnerability: Appears to struggle initially on categorized climbs (first Viveiro pass), requiring recovery efforts that expend energy before final moves.
key_patterns: Consistently infiltrates long-range breakaways, maintaining position through high-speed openings and regrouping efforts.
Delivers strong performances across multiple stages, securing both stage win and runner-up finish.
Attacks solo from breakaway groups rather than leading out sprints, isolating rivals psychologically.
Benefits when chasers neutralize each other through tactical standoffs instead of organized pursuit.
form_trajectory: positive: consecutive top results within single Grand Tour, showing both consistency and recovery ability across multi-stage efforts
